wcag_dictionary.description: All non-text content that is presented to the user has a text alternative that serves the equivalent purpose, except for the situations listed in WCAG 2.1.

act_rules.title: Image button has non-empty accessible name
Image has non-empty accessible name
Element marked as decorative is not exposed
SVG element with explicit role has non-empty accessible name

common_failures: Missing alt attribute on elements
Empty alt attribute on informational images
Alt text is not equivalent (e.g., 'image.jpg' instead of description)
SVG icons without aria-label or title
Image buttons without accessible name
CSS background images used for meaningful content
CAPTCHA without alternative text or audio alternative
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical — screen reader users cannot perceive the content of images, charts, icons, or image-based buttons without text alternatives.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Inspect each non-text element for a programmatic text alternative. Verify decorative images use alt='' and role='presentation'.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user provided an HTML element missing text alternatives. Generate an optimized code snippet adding appropriate semantic HTML (like 'alt' tags) or ARIA labels to satisfy WCAG 1.1.1.

wcag_dictionary.description: For prerecorded audio-only and prerecorded video-only media, a text or audio alternative is provided, unless the audio or video is itself an alternative for text.

act_rules.title: Audio element content has text alternative
Video element visual-only content has accessible alternative

common_failures: Podcast or audio clip without a transcript
Silent promotional video without a text description
Audio with only a title and no content transcript
Video description is too vague to be equivalent
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical for deaf or hard-of-hearing users who cannot access audio content, and blind users who cannot perceive silent video.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: For each audio-only or video-only element, verify a full text transcript or audio description is accessible nearby or linked.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user provided a media element (audio/video) lacking an alternative. Generate a semantic HTML snippet incorporating a <track> element or adjacent text transcript container to satisfy WCAG 1.2.1.

wcag_dictionary.description: Captions are provided for all prerecorded audio content in synchronized media, except when the media is a media alternative for text and is clearly labeled as such.

act_rules.title: Video element auditory content has captions

common_failures: Video does not have captions
Captions are auto-generated and uncorrected
Captions do not include speaker identification
Captions do not represent non-speech audio events (e.g., [applause])
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical for deaf users who depend on synchronized captions to follow video content.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Play the video and verify captions are synchronized, accurate, and include non-speech sounds and speaker identification.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user provided a <video> element without captions. Generate an HTML snippet adding a <track kind='captions'> element pointing to a valid VTT file to satisfy WCAG 1.2.2.

wcag_dictionary.description: An alternative for time-based media or an audio description of prerecorded video content is provided for synchronized media, except when the media is a media alternative for text.

act_rules.title: Video element visual content has audio description

common_failures: Video has meaningful visuals not described in the soundtrack
No link to a text alternative for the video
Audio description track exists but is not linked via <track>
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical for blind and low-vision users who cannot see on-screen actions, text overlays, or visual transitions in video.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Check if the video soundtrack describes all meaningful visual content. If not, verify an audio description track or full text alternative is available.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user provided a <video> element without an audio description. Generate an HTML snippet linking an audio description track using <track kind='descriptions'> to satisfy WCAG 1.2.3.

wcag_dictionary.description: Captions are provided for all live audio content in synchronized media.
common_failures: Live broadcast streams with no real-time captions
Webinars or video calls without live captioning service
Live news streams without captions
Auto-generated live captions that are so inaccurate they are not equivalent
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical for deaf and hard-of-hearing users who cannot access live audio content such as broadcasts, webinars, or live events.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: During a live broadcast or event, verify that synchronized captions appear with acceptable accuracy and latency. Auto-generated captions alone are typically insufficient unless accuracy is verified.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user has a live streaming media element without captions. Generate an HTML snippet referencing a live caption track and describe the server-side CART (Communication Access Realtime Translation) or live ASR captioning integration needed to satisfy WCAG 1.2.4.

wcag_dictionary.description: Audio description is provided for all prerecorded video content in synchronized media.

act_rules.title: Video element visual content has audio description

common_failures: Prerecorded video has meaningful visuals not described in the audio track
Audio description track exists but is not linked via <track kind='descriptions'>
Extended audio description is needed but not provided
Audio description is provided via a separate URL only, without an obvious link
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical for blind and low-vision users who depend on spoken descriptions of on-screen visual actions, text overlays, and scene changes.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Watch the video with eyes closed and verify the audio track (dialogue + description) fully conveys all meaningful visual information. Alternatively confirm a <track kind='descriptions'> element is present and links to a valid VTT description file.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user has a prerecorded <video> without an audio description track. Generate an HTML snippet adding a <track kind='descriptions' src='description.vtt'> element and explain the VTT format required to satisfy WCAG 1.2.5.

wcag_dictionary.description: Sign language interpretation is provided for all prerecorded audio content in synchronized media.
common_failures: Prerecorded video has no sign language interpretation track or inset
Sign language video is provided but not synchronised with the main video
Sign language interpreter is too small or poorly lit to follow
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical for deaf users whose primary language is a sign language and who have limited proficiency in written text.
affected_users: Deaf users who use sign language as their primary language
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Verify a sign language interpretation video track or picture-in-picture inset is provided and is synchronised with the audio content.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user has a prerecorded video without sign language interpretation. Describe how to embed a synchronised sign language video inset and generate the HTML structure to satisfy WCAG 1.2.6.

wcag_dictionary.description: Where pauses in foreground audio are insufficient to allow audio descriptions to convey the sense of the video, extended audio description is provided for all prerecorded video content in synchronized media.
common_failures: Video has continuous dialogue with no natural pauses for audio description
Standard audio description is truncated because pauses are too short
Extended audio description is not offered even when standard description is inadequate
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical for blind users watching content-dense videos where the standard audio description cannot fit all visual information into natural pauses.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Identify videos where pauses are too short for standard audio description. Verify an extended description version is provided that pauses the video to allow full description.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's video has continuous audio leaving no room for standard description. Explain the extended audio description technique and generate HTML to provide an extended-description version link to satisfy WCAG 1.2.7.

wcag_dictionary.description: An alternative for time-based media is provided for all prerecorded synchronised media and for all prerecorded video-only media.
common_failures: No full text transcript provided for prerecorded synchronised media
Transcript omits speaker identification or non-speech audio events
Transcript is paywalled or behind a separate login
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for deaf-blind users who access content via braille displays and require a full text alternative for all time-based media.
affected_users: Users who prefer reading over watching
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Verify a full text transcript is available for every piece of synchronised media, covering all dialogue, speaker identification, and significant non-speech sounds.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user has synchronised media without a full text transcript. Generate an HTML snippet with a linked or collapsible transcript section containing a complete text alternative to satisfy WCAG 1.2.8.

wcag_dictionary.description: An alternative for time-based media that presents equivalent information for live audio-only content is provided.
common_failures: Live radio stream or audio webcast with no real-time text alternative
Live podcast with no CART (Communication Access Realtime Translation) feed
Live audio transcript is delayed by more than a few seconds making it unusable
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical for deaf and hard-of-hearing users who cannot access live audio-only content such as radio or live audio streams.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: During a live audio-only broadcast, verify a real-time text alternative (e.g., CART transcript) is available and updated with acceptable latency.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user has a live audio-only stream without a text alternative. Describe CART integration options and generate HTML for embedding a live transcript feed alongside the audio player to satisfy WCAG 1.2.9.

wcag_dictionary.description: Information, structure, and relationships conveyed through presentation can be programmatically determined or are available in text.

act_rules.title: ARIA attribute is defined in WAI-ARIA
Table header cell has assigned cells
Headers attribute specified on a cell refers to cells in the same table

common_failures: Data tables without <th> or scope attributes
Using visual-only formatting (bold/color) to imply structure
Form labels associated only by proximity, not programmatically
Custom heading styled with CSS but not using <h1>–<h6>
List items not wrapped in <ul>, <ol>, or <dl>
Invalid or undefined ARIA attributes
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious — screen readers cannot understand the logical layout of tables, forms, and ARIA-based widgets when semantic structure is missing.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Examine HTML source for semantic elements. Validate that tables use <th> with scope, forms use <label for=>, and no structural meaning is conveyed through CSS alone.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user provided HTML with broken semantic relationships (like detached table headers or invalid ARIA). Generate a corrected HTML snippet establishing proper programmatic relationships (e.g., matching 'id' and 'headers' attributes) to satisfy WCAG 1.3.1.

wcag_dictionary.description: If the sequence in which content is presented affects its meaning, a correct reading sequence can be programmatically determined.
common_failures: Content laid out with CSS absolute positioning in a different DOM order than visual order
Using HTML tables for layout, creating a confusing reading sequence
JavaScript dynamically inserts content in a location that differs from its visual position
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious — screen readers read content in DOM order, which may be completely different from the visual order when CSS positioning is misused.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Disable CSS and verify the content still reads in a logical order that preserves meaning.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user has content that displays visually in one order but exists in the DOM in a different order. Generate corrected HTML/CSS that aligns DOM reading sequence with visual order to satisfy WCAG 1.3.2.

wcag_dictionary.description: Instructions for understanding and operating content do not rely solely on sensory characteristics of components such as shape, color, size, visual location, orientation, or sound.
common_failures: Instructions say 'click the green button' without naming the button
Instructions say 'see the diagram on the right' without describing it
Error messages say 'the field highlighted in red is required'
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical for users who are colorblind, blind, or deaf, who cannot rely on sensory cues alone.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Check all instructions for sensory-only references. Verify each reference includes a non-sensory identifier (text label, location name, etc.).
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user has instructions that rely on color or shape alone to identify UI elements. Generate revised copy and HTML that adds non-sensory labels to satisfy WCAG 1.3.3.

wcag_dictionary.description: Content does not restrict its view and operation to a single display orientation, such as portrait or landscape, unless a specific display orientation is essential.

act_rules.title: Orientation of the page is not restricted using CSS transforms

common_failures: CSS or JavaScript locks the viewport to portrait mode
App uses screen.orientation.lock() without an essential use case
Content becomes unusable or hidden when rotated
wcag_dictionary.impact: Moderate — users with devices mounted in a fixed orientation (e.g., wheelchair-mounted) cannot rotate to landscape if the page restricts it.
affected_users: Users with motor disabilities using fixed-mounted devices
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Rotate the device or emulate landscape/portrait in DevTools. Verify all content is accessible and functional in both orientations.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user has CSS that restricts page orientation. Generate corrected CSS removing orientation locks to satisfy WCAG 1.3.4.

wcag_dictionary.description: The purpose of each input field that collects information about the user can be programmatically determined when the input serves a purpose identified in the Input Purposes for User Interface Components section.

act_rules.title: Autocomplete attribute has valid value

common_failures: Name, email, phone, or address fields missing autocomplete attributes
autocomplete='off' used on personal data fields
Invalid or misspelled autocomplete token values
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for users with cognitive disabilities and motor impairments who rely on browsers and password managers to auto-fill personal data.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Inspect form inputs for valid autocomplete tokens. Cross-reference with the WCAG 1.3.5 token list (name, email, tel, street-address, etc.).
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user provided a form input missing purpose identification. Generate a code snippet adding the correct HTML5 'autocomplete' attribute token for the requested input type to satisfy WCAG 1.3.5.

wcag_dictionary.description: In content implemented using markup languages, the purpose of user interface components, icons, and regions can be programmatically determined.
common_failures: Custom icons have no semantic meaning exposed via ARIA
Page regions lack landmark roles (main, nav, aside, header, footer)
UI components use non-standard roles that cannot be mapped by personalisation tools
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for users with cognitive disabilities who use personalisation tools that rely on semantic purpose to substitute familiar symbols or simplify the interface.
affected_users: Users using AAC (Augmentative and Alternative Communication) tools
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Inspect all icons and UI regions. Verify each has a programmatically determinable purpose via ARIA roles, landmark elements, or standard HTML semantics.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user has icons and regions without semantic purpose exposed in markup. Generate HTML with appropriate landmark roles and ARIA attributes to satisfy WCAG 1.3.6.

wcag_dictionary.description: Color is not used as the only visual means of conveying information, indicating an action, prompting a response, or distinguishing a visual element.
common_failures: Required form fields indicated only by a red border
Links distinguished from surrounding text only by color
Charts use only color to differentiate data series
Error states conveyed only through red text
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious — colorblind users and users with low vision may completely miss information conveyed only by color differences.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Convert the page to grayscale (DevTools or browser extension) and verify all information and distinctions remain perceptible.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user is using color alone to distinguish UI states. Generate HTML/CSS that adds a secondary indicator (icon, pattern, underline, text label) to satisfy WCAG 1.4.1.

wcag_dictionary.description: If any audio on a web page plays automatically for more than 3 seconds, either a mechanism is available to pause or stop the audio, or a mechanism is available to control audio volume independently from the overall system volume.
common_failures: Background music auto-plays on page load with no pause control
Video auto-plays with audio and the only control is system volume
Audio advertisement plays automatically with no stop mechanism
Audio plays for more than 3 seconds before any control is reachable via keyboard
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical for screen reader users whose speech output is drowned out by auto-playing audio, and for users with cognitive or attention disorders.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Load the page and check if any audio plays automatically beyond 3 seconds. Verify a clearly labelled pause/stop control or independent volume control is present and is the first focusable element on the page or reachable within 3 keystrokes.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user has auto-playing background audio with no control. Generate an HTML/JS snippet adding an accessible play/pause toggle button as the first interactive element on the page to satisfy WCAG 1.4.2.

wcag_dictionary.description: The visual presentation of text and images of text has a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1, except for large text (3:1), incidental text, or logotypes.

act_rules.title: Text has minimum contrast

common_failures: Light grey text on white backgrounds
Placeholder text with insufficient contrast
Disabled element text that is unreadable
Colored text on similarly-colored backgrounds
Text overlaid on semi-transparent images
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical for users with low vision, color blindness, or those in high-glare environments.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Use a contrast analyzer tool (e.g., WebAIM Contrast Checker) with the exact foreground/background hex values. Test all text states including hover and focus.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user provided CSS with a text foreground and background color combination that fails contrast minimums. Generate contrast-compliant CSS modifying the hex codes to reach a 4.5:1 ratio to satisfy WCAG 1.4.3.

wcag_dictionary.description: Text can be resized without assistive technology up to 200 percent without loss of content or functionality.
common_failures: Viewport meta tag prevents user scaling
Fixed pixel heights on containers clip text at larger sizes
Text uses px units that don't scale with browser font size
Content overflows or overlaps at 200% zoom
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious — users with low vision who increase browser font size or zoom must be able to use all content without horizontal scrolling or content clipping.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Zoom the browser to 200% and verify no text is cut off, no horizontal scrollbar appears for vertical content, and all functionality remains operable.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user has a viewport meta tag with user-scalable=no or CSS with fixed-height containers that clip text. Generate corrected code allowing text resize to 200% to satisfy WCAG 1.4.4.

wcag_dictionary.description: If the technologies being used can achieve the visual presentation, text is used to convey information rather than images of text, except for customizable and essential images of text.
common_failures: Logos or banners contain text rendered as a JPEG or PNG
Infographic body text is baked into an image
Charts with axis labels embedded in the image rather than in HTML
Screenshots of code or terminal output used as primary content
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious — images of text cannot be resized, recoloured, or adapted by user stylesheets, harming low-vision and dyslexic users.
exceptions: Images of text where the visual presentation is essential to the information conveyed
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Inspect content areas for images that contain text. Verify the same information is available as real HTML text, or confirm it falls under a defined exception (logotype or essential).
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user is using an image to display a text heading or label. Generate an HTML/CSS snippet replacing the image of text with real HTML text styled to achieve the same visual appearance, satisfying WCAG 1.4.5.

wcag_dictionary.description: The visual presentation of text and images of text has a contrast ratio of at least 7:1, except for large text (4.5:1), incidental text, and logotypes.
common_failures: Body text with a contrast ratio between 4.5:1 and 7:1
Large text with a contrast ratio between 3:1 and 4.5:1
Placeholder or hint text below 7:1 contrast
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical for users with severe low vision who require higher contrast than the AA minimum to read text.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Measure contrast ratios using a colour contrast analyser. Verify normal text meets 7:1 and large text meets 4.5:1.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user needs AAA contrast compliance. Generate CSS with foreground and background colour combinations that achieve at least 7:1 contrast for normal text to satisfy WCAG 1.4.6.

wcag_dictionary.description: For prerecorded audio-only content that contains primarily speech in the foreground, is not an audio CAPTCHA or audio logo, and is not vocalization intended to be primarily musical expression, background audio is either absent or at least 20 dB lower than the foreground speech.
common_failures: Podcast with background music at the same volume as the narrator
Educational audio with background sounds louder than 20 dB below speech
Interview recording with ambient noise competing with speech
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for users with hearing loss or auditory processing disorders who struggle to separate speech from background noise.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Analyse the audio file using audio editing software. Verify background audio is at least 20 dB lower than foreground speech, or is absent entirely.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's audio content has background noise too close in volume to the speech. Provide guidance on audio production techniques and HTML controls that allow users to access a speech-only version to satisfy WCAG 1.4.7.

wcag_dictionary.description: For the visual presentation of blocks of text, a mechanism is available to achieve: foreground and background colours chosen by the user; width no more than 80 characters; text not fully justified; line spacing at least 1.5 within paragraphs and paragraph spacing at least 1.5 times the line spacing; text resizable up to 200% without assistive technology.
common_failures: Text is full-justified causing uneven word spacing (rivers of white space)
No mechanism to change text and background colours
Content columns wider than 80 characters with no way to reduce width
Line spacing locked below 1.5 via !important

requirements.foreground_background_choice: User can select foreground and background colours
requirements.max_width: No more than 80 characters or glyphs per line

wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for users with dyslexia and cognitive disabilities who need control over text presentation.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Verify text alignment is not justify. Check column widths, line spacing, and that a mechanism (theme switcher or CSS override) allows users to set custom colours.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's body text uses text-align:justify and has no colour customisation mechanism. Generate CSS removing full justification, constraining line width, and adding appropriate spacing to satisfy WCAG 1.4.8.

wcag_dictionary.description: Images of text are only used for pure decoration or where a particular presentation of text is essential to the information being conveyed.
common_failures: Navigation labels rendered as images with no essential reason
Headings or pull-quotes saved as PNGs instead of HTML text
Stylised text in banners that could be replicated with CSS
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious — stricter than 1.4.5, allowing no exceptions except pure decoration or essential presentation.
exceptions: Essential: where the specific visual rendering is essential to convey meaning (e.g., a font specimen)
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Identify all images of text. Verify each is either purely decorative or its specific visual appearance is essential. Replace all others with real HTML text + CSS.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user is using images of text for non-essential content. Generate HTML/CSS replacing every non-essential image of text with real styled text to satisfy WCAG 1.4.9.

wcag_dictionary.description: Content can be presented without loss of information or functionality, and without requiring scrolling in two dimensions, at a width equivalent to 320 CSS pixels.
common_failures: Fixed-width layouts that require horizontal scrolling at 320px
Data tables that overflow without a scrollable container
Images or media with fixed widths wider than the viewport
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious — users who zoom to 400% (equivalent to 320px viewport width) need to avoid two-dimensional scrolling.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Set the viewport to 320px wide and verify all content reflows into a single column without horizontal scrolling (except for content that inherently requires 2D layout, like data tables or maps).
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user has a fixed-width layout that breaks at 320px viewport. Generate responsive CSS using flexible units and media queries to satisfy WCAG 1.4.10.

wcag_dictionary.description: The visual presentation of UI components and graphical objects has a contrast ratio of at least 3:1 against adjacent color(s).
common_failures: Form input borders are too low contrast against the background
Icon buttons without background have insufficient contrast
Charts or graphs with low-contrast lines or bars
Focus indicators below 3:1 contrast
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious — users with low vision may not be able to identify form inputs, buttons, icons, or chart elements.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Measure the contrast ratio of UI control boundaries (e.g., checkbox border vs background). Use a contrast tool with the component's edge color and its adjacent background color.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user has UI components or icons with insufficient non-text contrast. Generate corrected CSS changing border or fill colors to achieve at least 3:1 contrast to satisfy WCAG 1.4.11.

wcag_dictionary.description: No loss of content or functionality occurs by setting: line height to at least 1.5x font size, letter spacing to at least 0.12x font size, word spacing to at least 0.16x font size, and spacing after paragraphs to at least 2x font size.

act_rules.title: Important letter spacing in style attributes is wide enough
Important line height in style attributes is wide enough
Important word spacing in style attributes is wide enough

common_failures: Inline style uses !important to lock letter-spacing
Fixed-height containers clip text when line-height is increased
Overflow: hidden on components that do not account for extra spacing
word-spacing or letter-spacing forced via !important in stylesheets
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for users with dyslexia and cognitive disabilities who rely on custom user stylesheets or browser extensions to override text spacing.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Apply a bookmarklet or user stylesheet overriding spacing to the WCAG minimums. Verify no content is clipped, overlapped, or functionally broken.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user provided CSS that restricts text spacing using '!important'. Generate a corrected CSS snippet removing the strict spacing overrides or adjusting them to meet WCAG 1.4.12 minimums.

wcag_dictionary.description: Where receiving and then removing pointer hover or keyboard focus triggers additional content to become visible and then hidden, the additional content is dismissible, hoverable, and persistent.
common_failures: Tooltip disappears as soon as the pointer moves off the trigger
Tooltip cannot be dismissed by pressing Escape without moving focus
Tooltip obscures underlying content and cannot be moved away
Hoverable content auto-dismisses before the user can read or interact with it

requirements.dismissible: The user can dismiss the additional content without moving pointer hover or keyboard focus (e.g., Escape key).
requirements.hoverable: If a pointer triggered the content, the pointer can be moved over the additional content without it disappearing.
requirements.persistent: The content remains visible until the user removes hover/focus, dismisses it, or the information is no longer valid.

wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for users with low vision who zoom in and need to move the pointer over tooltip content, and for users with cognitive disabilities who need time to read tooltip text.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Trigger all tooltips and popovers via keyboard focus and mouse hover. Verify: (1) Escape dismisses the content, (2) moving the pointer into the tooltip does not close it, (3) the content stays open until intentionally dismissed.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's tooltip disappears on mouseout and cannot be accessed via keyboard. Generate an HTML/CSS/JS snippet implementing a hover/focus tooltip that is dismissible, hoverable, and persistent to satisfy WCAG 1.4.13.

wcag_dictionary.description: All functionality of the content is operable through a keyboard interface without requiring specific timings for individual keystrokes, except where the underlying function requires input that depends on the path of the user's movement.

act_rules.title: Focusable element has no keyboard trap via standard navigation
Scrollable content can be reached with sequential focus navigation

common_failures: Custom dropdown or modal traps keyboard focus
onClick-only handlers without onKeyDown/onKeyPress equivalents
draggable elements without keyboard alternatives
Canvas or SVG interactions only available via mouse
Scrollable region not reachable via Tab key
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical — blocks all users who rely solely on keyboard navigation, including those with motor disabilities, switch access users, and power keyboard users.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Navigate the entire page using only Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter, Space, and arrow keys. Verify all interactive elements are reachable and operable.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user provided an interactive DOM element that traps focus or cannot be reached via keyboard. Generate a modified HTML/JS snippet adding tabindex='0' and necessary keyboard event listeners (Enter/Space) to satisfy WCAG 2.1.1.

wcag_dictionary.description: If keyboard focus can be moved to a component using a keyboard interface, then focus can be moved away from that component using only a keyboard interface.

act_rules.title: Focusable element has no keyboard trap via standard navigation

common_failures: Modal dialog does not return focus on Escape
Date picker or custom widget captures Tab and never releases
Embedded iframe traps focus indefinitely
Third-party widget without a documented focus escape method
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical — a keyboard trap completely prevents a user from continuing to navigate the page.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Tab into every interactive component. Verify that pressing Escape or following documented instructions always allows the user to move focus away.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's modal or widget traps keyboard focus. Generate a JavaScript focus management snippet that constrains focus within the modal but returns it to the trigger element on close, satisfying WCAG 2.1.2.

wcag_dictionary.description: All functionality of the content is operable through a keyboard interface without requiring specific timings for individual keystrokes.
common_failures: Drawing or free-hand input tools without a keyboard-driven alternative mode
Drag-and-drop interfaces with no keyboard reordering mechanism
Gesture-only controls on touch interfaces without keyboard equivalents
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical — stricter than 2.1.1, removing the path-dependent movement exception, requiring all functionality including drawing to be keyboard operable.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Attempt to perform every single function using only keyboard. Verify no functionality — including drawing, resizing, or path-based interaction — requires a pointing device.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user has drawing or drag interactions with no keyboard alternative. Generate a JavaScript snippet implementing keyboard-driven equivalents (arrow keys for movement, Enter for drop) to satisfy WCAG 2.1.3.

wcag_dictionary.description: If a keyboard shortcut is implemented in content using only letter, punctuation, number, or symbol characters, then a mechanism is available to turn off, remap, or restrict activation to when the component has focus.
common_failures: Single-character shortcut keys active globally that interfere with voice input
App binds letter keys (e.g., 'f' for favourite) with no way to disable them
Keyboard shortcuts conflict with screen reader or OS shortcuts
No settings panel or mechanism to remap or deactivate shortcuts
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for voice input users (e.g., Dragon NaturallySpeaking) where dictating text can accidentally trigger single-key shortcuts.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Identify all single-character keyboard shortcuts. Verify a mechanism exists to: (a) turn them off, (b) remap them to include a modifier key (Ctrl, Alt, etc.), or (c) restrict them to only activate when the relevant component has focus.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's application uses single-character keyboard shortcuts globally. Generate a JavaScript snippet implementing a settings toggle to disable or remap these shortcuts to require a modifier key, satisfying WCAG 2.1.4.

wcag_dictionary.description: For each time limit that is set by the content, the user can turn off, adjust, or extend the time limit, with specific exceptions for real-time events and essential time limits.

act_rules.title: Meta element has no refresh delay

common_failures: <meta http-equiv='refresh' content='30'> with no warning
Session timeout with no extension prompt
Auto-advancing slideshow with no pause control
Form submission window with a fixed countdown
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious — users with cognitive disabilities, reading impairments, or motor difficulties need extra time and must not be timed out unexpectedly.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Identify all time limits. Verify each provides at least one of: turn off, adjust, or extend (at least 10× default time and at least 20 seconds to request extension).
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user provided HTML using a meta refresh tag for a time limit. Generate alternative logic (like a JavaScript prompt allowing the user to extend the session) to satisfy WCAG 2.2.1.

wcag_dictionary.description: For moving, blinking, scrolling, or auto-updating information, the user can pause, stop, or hide it, unless the movement is essential.
common_failures: Animated carousel with no pause button
Ticker or marquee scrolling text with no stop mechanism
Auto-updating news feed with no pause control
Animated background that cannot be stopped
Loading spinner or animation that plays indefinitely with no way to disable
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical for users with attention disorders, cognitive disabilities, and photosensitive conditions who are distracted or harmed by persistent motion.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Identify all moving, blinking, scrolling, or auto-updating content. Verify a clearly labelled pause, stop, or hide control is available and operable via keyboard.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user has an auto-advancing carousel or scrolling ticker without a pause control. Generate an HTML/JS snippet adding an accessible pause/play toggle button that halts and resumes animation to satisfy WCAG 2.2.2.

wcag_dictionary.description: Timing is not an essential part of the event or activity presented by the content, except for non-interactive synchronised media and real-time events.
common_failures: Quiz or test with a mandatory countdown timer that cannot be removed
Auction interface requiring bids within a fixed window
Any form with a time limit that cannot be turned off entirely
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical for users who need unlimited time — those with cognitive disabilities, motor impairments, or reading difficulties.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Verify that no time limits exist in the content except for real-time events or synchronised media.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's application has mandatory time limits. Describe how to architect the experience without timing constraints and generate UI that removes all time-dependent mechanics to satisfy WCAG 2.2.3.

wcag_dictionary.description: Interruptions can be postponed or suppressed by the user, except for interruptions involving an emergency.
common_failures: Push notifications or alerts cannot be deferred
Live chat widgets interrupt the user without a suppression option
Auto-updating content interrupts the current task with no 'do not disturb' setting
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for users with attention disorders and cognitive disabilities who are disrupted by unexpected interruptions.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Identify all system-initiated interruptions (alerts, notifications, updates). Verify each can be postponed or suppressed by the user.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's app has non-suppressible notifications. Generate a JavaScript notification manager with a user-controlled 'do not disturb' toggle to satisfy WCAG 2.2.4.

wcag_dictionary.description: When an authenticated session expires, the user can continue the activity without loss of data after re-authenticating.
common_failures: Session timeout clears a partially completed form without warning
Re-authentication redirects to an empty form losing all entered data
Shopping cart is cleared after session expiry
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for users who need extended time to complete tasks — data loss after re-authentication is particularly harmful for users with cognitive or motor disabilities.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Allow a session to expire mid-form. Re-authenticate and verify all previously entered data is preserved and the task can be resumed.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's app loses form data on session expiry. Generate a JavaScript snippet saving form state to sessionStorage and restoring it after re-authentication to satisfy WCAG 2.2.5.

wcag_dictionary.description: Users are warned of the duration of any user inactivity that could cause data loss, unless the data is preserved for more than 20 hours when the user does not take any actions.
common_failures: Session timeout occurs without any prior warning to the user
Warning is shown but does not state the exact duration before timeout
Data is not preserved after timeout and no warning was given
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for users with cognitive disabilities who may lose track of time and inadvertently let sessions expire, losing work.
affected_users: Screen reader users who navigate slowly
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Identify inactivity timeouts. Verify the user is warned of the timeout duration upfront (not just when it is about to expire) unless data is preserved for 20+ hours.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's app has an undisclosed inactivity timeout. Generate a UI notification displayed on page load that clearly states the session duration and warns before expiry to satisfy WCAG 2.2.6.

wcag_dictionary.description: Web pages do not contain anything that flashes more than three times in any one second period, or the flash is below the general and red flash thresholds.
common_failures: Animated GIFs or video with rapid flashing sequences
Loading animations with rapid strobe effects
JavaScript-driven blinking content exceeding 3 Hz
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical — content flashing faster than 3 Hz can trigger photosensitive epileptic seizures.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Use the Photosensitive Epilepsy Analysis Tool (PEAT) to analyze videos and animations for flash rates exceeding the threshold.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user has a rapidly flashing animation. Generate revised CSS/JS that limits animation to below 3 Hz or provides a prefers-reduced-motion media query to satisfy WCAG 2.3.1.

wcag_dictionary.description: Web pages do not contain anything that flashes more than three times in any one second period.
common_failures: Any content flashing more than 3 times per second regardless of size or area
Animations with rapid strobe effects below the general flash threshold but still above 3 Hz
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical — stricter than 2.3.1 (removes the threshold exception), ensuring no content flashes more than 3 times per second under any conditions.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Use PEAT (Photosensitive Epilepsy Analysis Tool) to verify no content flashes more than 3 times per second, regardless of its size or colour.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user has flashing content that falls under the 2.3.1 threshold but still flashes more than 3 times per second. Generate CSS or JS limiting the animation rate to at most 3 Hz to satisfy WCAG 2.3.2.

wcag_dictionary.description: Motion animation triggered by interaction can be disabled, unless the animation is essential to the functionality or the information being conveyed.
common_failures: Parallax scrolling effects with no option to disable
Page transition animations with no reduced-motion support
Zoom or spin effects on interactive elements ignoring prefers-reduced-motion
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for users with vestibular disorders (e.g., Ménière's disease) for whom motion animations can trigger dizziness, nausea, or migraines.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Enable the OS prefers-reduced-motion setting. Verify all non-essential motion animations are disabled or significantly reduced.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's site has parallax or transition animations. Generate a CSS media query using prefers-reduced-motion to disable all non-essential motion animations to satisfy WCAG 2.3.3.

wcag_dictionary.description: A mechanism is available to bypass blocks of content that are repeated on multiple web pages.
common_failures: No skip navigation link before the main content area
Skip link exists but is not keyboard focusable
Skip link target anchor is missing from the DOM
No landmark regions (main, nav, header) for screen reader navigation
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious — keyboard and screen reader users must Tab through every navigation item on every page load without a skip mechanism.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Tab to the page. Verify the first focusable element is a visible or visually-hidden 'Skip to main content' link. Activate it and confirm focus moves to main content.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's page lacks a skip navigation link. Generate an HTML snippet with a visually-hidden skip link that becomes visible on focus and targets the main content landmark to satisfy WCAG 2.4.1.

wcag_dictionary.description: Web pages have titles that describe their topic or purpose.

act_rules.title: HTML page has non-empty title
HTML page title is descriptive

common_failures: Missing <title> element entirely
Empty <title> tag
Generic title like 'Untitled Document' or 'Page 1'
Title does not change between pages in a single-page application
Title duplicates the site name without describing the page
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious — screen reader users rely on the page title announced on load to orient themselves; tab users need it to identify browser tabs.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Inspect the <head> element. Verify a non-empty <title> exists that uniquely describes the page topic or purpose.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user provided an HTML document missing a descriptive title. Generate an optimized <title> tag snippet describing the page content to be placed in the <head> to satisfy WCAG 2.4.2.

wcag_dictionary.description: If a web page can be navigated sequentially and the navigation sequences affect meaning or operation, focusable components receive focus in an order that preserves meaning and operability.

act_rules.title: Interactive elements have a sequential focus order that reflects the visual layout

common_failures: Positive tabindex values create a separate, confusing focus order
Modal dialogs that open without moving focus into the dialog
DOM order differs dramatically from visual layout
Focus jumps unpredictably after a user action
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious — a confusing focus order disorients keyboard and screen reader users who depend on logical sequential navigation.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Tab through the page and verify focus order follows the visual and logical reading sequence. Check that no positive tabindex values are used.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's page uses positive tabindex values that disrupt natural focus order. Generate corrected HTML removing positive tabindexes and reorganizing DOM order to match visual order, satisfying WCAG 2.4.3.

wcag_dictionary.description: The purpose of each link can be determined from the link text alone or from the link text together with its programmatically determined link context, except where the purpose of the link would be ambiguous to users in general.

act_rules.title: Link has non-empty accessible name
Link in context is descriptive

common_failures: Link text is 'Click here', 'Read more', 'Learn more', or 'More'
Multiple links with identical text going to different destinations
Icon-only links with no accessible name
URL used as link text (e.g., https://example.com/page?id=123)
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious — screen reader users often navigate via a list of links read out of context; generic link text makes this impossible.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Pull a list of all links using a screen reader's link list feature (e.g., NVDA: Insert+F7). Verify each link's purpose is clear without surrounding context.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user provided a link with vague text (like 'click here'). Generate an HTML snippet with descriptive link text or an 'aria-label' that clearly defines the link's destination to satisfy WCAG 2.4.4.

wcag_dictionary.description: More than one way is available to locate a web page within a set of web pages, except where the web page is the result of, or a step in, a process.
common_failures: Site provides only a navigation menu with no search or sitemap
Single-path navigation with no alternative access route
Related links section is absent and no site search exists
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for users with cognitive disabilities who may not remember the navigation path and need search or a sitemap as an alternative.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Verify at least two of the following exist: navigation menu, search function, sitemap, table of contents, or related-links section.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's site has only one navigation method. Generate HTML for a site search widget and a sitemap link to provide multiple ways to find content, satisfying WCAG 2.4.5.

wcag_dictionary.description: Headings and labels describe topic or purpose.
common_failures: Headings are generic (e.g., 'Section 1', 'Details')
Form labels say only 'Field 1', 'Field 2'
Multiple sections share the same heading text
Labels are present but do not describe the input purpose
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for screen reader users who navigate by headings and for users with cognitive disabilities who rely on descriptive labels to understand page structure.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Review all headings and labels. Verify each is descriptive and unique where necessary. Use a screen reader heading-list feature to check that heading text alone communicates page structure.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user has vague headings and labels. Generate corrected HTML with descriptive heading text and input labels that clearly convey topic and purpose to satisfy WCAG 2.4.6.

wcag_dictionary.description: Any keyboard operable user interface has a mode of operation where the keyboard focus indicator is visible.

act_rules.title: Element in sequential focus order has visible focus

common_failures: outline: none or outline: 0 in CSS with no replacement indicator
Focus styles removed in a global CSS reset without being re-added
Custom focus style is too subtle to see (e.g., color change only)
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical for sighted keyboard users who rely on a visible cursor to know where they are on the page.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Tab through all interactive elements. Verify a visible focus indicator (outline, border, underline, background change) is present at every stop.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user provided CSS that removes focus outlines (e.g., outline: none). Generate a contrast-compliant CSS snippet utilizing the :focus or :focus-visible pseudo-classes to restore focus indicators and satisfy WCAG 2.4.7.

wcag_dictionary.description: Information about the user's location within a set of web pages is available.
common_failures: Multi-level site with no breadcrumb or site map
Step-based process with no step indicator
No visual indication of the current page in the navigation menu
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for users with cognitive disabilities who need orientation cues to understand where they are within a site hierarchy.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Navigate to a deep page. Verify the current location is indicated via breadcrumb, highlighted nav item, or equivalent mechanism.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's site has no breadcrumb or location indicator. Generate an accessible HTML breadcrumb component using <nav aria-label='Breadcrumb'> and an ordered list to satisfy WCAG 2.4.8.

wcag_dictionary.description: A mechanism is available to allow the purpose of each link to be identified from link text alone, except where the purpose of the link would be ambiguous to users in general.
common_failures: Link text is 'Read more' with no aria-label providing context
Multiple 'Download' links that are indistinguishable from link text alone
Icon-only links with no accessible name
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious — stricter than 2.4.4, requiring each link to be self-describing from its text alone, without relying on surrounding context.
affected_users: Screen reader users navigating via link lists
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Pull up the screen reader link list (e.g., NVDA Insert+F7). Every link must convey its destination or purpose from its text alone with no surrounding context needed.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user has links that rely on context. Generate HTML replacing ambiguous link text with fully descriptive text or aria-labels that are self-sufficient to satisfy WCAG 2.4.9.

wcag_dictionary.description: Section headings are used to organise the content.
common_failures: Long articles or documents with no internal section headings
Content sections separated only by visual whitespace with no heading
Heading hierarchy skips levels (h1 → h3) breaking navigation
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for screen reader users who navigate long pages by jumping between headings and for users with cognitive disabilities who need visible structure.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Check all long-form content for section headings. Verify the heading hierarchy is logical and that no content block exceeds a reasonable length without a heading.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's long-form content has no section headings. Generate a structured HTML document skeleton with a logical heading hierarchy (h1–h4) organising the content to satisfy WCAG 2.4.10.

wcag_dictionary.description: When a user interface component receives keyboard focus, the focus indicator has an area of at least the perimeter of the unfocused component times CSS pixels in size, a contrast ratio of at least 3:1 between focused and unfocused states, and is not entirely hidden by author-created content. (New in WCAG 2.2)
common_failures: Focus indicator is a 1px dotted border that does not meet minimum area
Focus indicator color change alone does not meet 3:1 contrast ratio
Focus ring is clipped or hidden by overflow:hidden on a parent element
Tooltip or dropdown obscures the focus indicator of the trigger element

requirements.minimum_area: At least the perimeter of the component × 2 CSS pixels (e.g., a 100×30px button requires a focus indicator area of ≥260 CSS pixels²)
requirements.contrast: At least 3:1 between the focused and unfocused state pixels
requirements.not_hidden: The focus indicator must not be entirely hidden by author-created content

wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical for sighted keyboard users who need a clearly visible, high-contrast focus indicator to track their position on the page. Stricter than WCAG 2.4.7.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Tab to each interactive element and measure the focus indicator area and contrast using DevTools or a colour contrast tool. Verify neither criterion falls below the minimum.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's focus indicator fails the WCAG 2.2 minimum area or contrast requirements. Generate CSS using :focus-visible with a solid high-contrast outline of at least 2px that meets both the area and 3:1 contrast requirements to satisfy WCAG 2.4.11.

wcag_dictionary.description: When a user interface component receives keyboard focus, the component is not entirely hidden due to author-created content. (New in WCAG 2.2)
common_failures: Sticky header completely covers the focused element when tabbing
Cookie banner overlaps the bottom of the page hiding focused links
Fixed chat widget covers focused elements in the lower viewport
Dropdown menu appears and fully obscures the trigger element's focus indicator
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for keyboard users who lose track of their position when the focused element is hidden behind sticky headers, footers, or overlays.
wcag_dictionary.note: New in WCAG 2.2. A component is 'not entirely hidden' — partial obscurement is a failure only for the stricter 2.4.13 (AAA).
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Tab through all interactive elements. Verify no focused component is entirely hidden behind a sticky header, footer, modal, or other author-created overlay.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's sticky header covers focused elements. Generate CSS using scroll-margin-top or scroll-padding-top on the html element to offset focus scroll position, satisfying WCAG 2.4.12.

wcag_dictionary.description: When a user interface component receives keyboard focus, no part of the component is hidden due to author-created content. (New in WCAG 2.2)
common_failures: Any part of a focused component is obscured by a sticky header or footer
Even partial overlap of the focused element by an overlay
Tooltip triggered by focus partially covers the focused element
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical for low-vision users who zoom and need the entire focused component to be visible.
wcag_dictionary.note: New in WCAG 2.2. Stricter than 2.4.12 (AA) — requires the entire focused component to be unobscured, not just partially visible.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Tab through all elements. Verify no part of any focused component is hidden by any author-created content including sticky headers, footers, toasts, or tooltips.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's fixed UI elements partially obscure focused components. Generate CSS ensuring focused elements are fully visible, including using scroll-margin offsets or adjusting z-index stacking, to satisfy WCAG 2.4.13.

wcag_dictionary.description: All functionality that uses multipoint or path-based gestures for operation can be operated with a single pointer without a path-based gesture, unless a multipoint or path-based gesture is essential.
common_failures: Map or image viewer only supports pinch-to-zoom with no +/- buttons
Carousel only advances via swipe with no arrow buttons
Drawing tool requires path-based gestures with no keyboard alternative
Slider requires dragging with no increment/decrement keyboard support
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical for users who cannot perform multipoint (two-finger) or path-based (swipe) gestures due to motor disabilities.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Identify all gestures used in the interface. For each multipoint or path-based gesture, verify an equivalent single-pointer alternative (button click, tap) achieves the same result.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's interface relies on swipe or pinch gestures without alternatives. Generate an HTML/JS snippet adding single-pointer alternative controls (e.g., Previous/Next buttons for a swipe carousel) to satisfy WCAG 2.5.1.

wcag_dictionary.description: For functionality that can be operated using a single pointer, at least one of the following is true: no down-event, abort/undo, up reversal, or essential.

act_rules.title: Activation of user interface components is on the up-event

common_failures: Button action triggers on mousedown/pointerdown instead of mouseup/pointerup
Drag-and-drop operation cannot be cancelled by releasing outside the drop zone
Accidental tap on a destructive action completes immediately on down-event

requirements.no_down_event: The down-event is not used to execute any part of the function.
requirements.abort_undo: Completion of the function is on the up-event and a mechanism is available to abort before completion or undo after.
requirements.up_reversal: The up-event reverses any outcome of the preceding down-event.
requirements.essential: Completing the function on the down-event is essential (e.g., a piano keyboard or game).

wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for users with motor disabilities and tremors who may accidentally activate controls and need a way to cancel accidental activations.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Press and hold on interactive controls. Verify the action does not trigger until the pointer is released (up-event). Drag the pointer off the control before releasing and verify the action is cancelled.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's button triggers on mousedown. Generate a JavaScript snippet changing the event listener from mousedown/pointerdown to mouseup/pointerup (or click) to satisfy WCAG 2.5.2.

wcag_dictionary.description: For user interface components with labels that include text or images of text, the accessible name contains the text that is presented visually.

act_rules.title: Visible label is part of accessible name

common_failures: aria-label overrides visible button text with a completely different string
Icon + text button where aria-label only describes the icon
Visible label is 'Search' but aria-label is 'Find content'
aria-labelledby points to an element with different text
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious — voice control users (e.g., Dragon NaturallySpeaking) activate elements by speaking their visible label; a mismatch makes the control inoperable by voice.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: For each interactive element with visible text, compare the visible text to the accessible name (computed using browser DevTools Accessibility panel). The accessible name must contain the visible text.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user provided a UI component where the ARIA label contradicts the visual text. Generate a corrected HTML snippet where the programmatic 'aria-label' matches or starts with the visible text to satisfy WCAG 2.5.3.

wcag_dictionary.description: Functionality that can be operated by device motion or user motion can also be operated by user interface components, and the response to the motion can be disabled to prevent accidental actuation.
common_failures: Shake to undo is the only way to undo an action
Tilting the device to scroll with no button alternative
Gyroscope-based controls with no on-screen equivalent
No setting to disable motion-activated features
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for users who have their device mounted in a fixed position or who have involuntary movement disorders (e.g., tremors) that could accidentally trigger motion-based interactions.
affected_users: Users with devices in fixed mounts
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Identify all motion-actuated features. Verify each has an equivalent UI control, and verify a setting exists to disable motion actuation entirely.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's app uses device motion (shake/tilt) as the only trigger for a function. Generate a JavaScript snippet adding an on-screen button alternative and a toggle to disable motion events to satisfy WCAG 2.5.4.

wcag_dictionary.description: The size of the target for pointer inputs is at least 44 by 44 CSS pixels, except where the target is in a sentence or its size is essential.
common_failures: Icon buttons smaller than 44×44 CSS pixels
Checkboxes or radio buttons with a hit area below 44×44 px
Close buttons on modals or toasts smaller than 44×44 px
Inline text links in dense navigation menus with small hit areas
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical for users with motor disabilities, tremors, or limited dexterity who need larger tap/click targets.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Inspect all interactive targets using DevTools. Verify each has a bounding box of at least 44×44 CSS pixels.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user has small interactive targets. Generate CSS increasing the clickable area of buttons and links to at least 44×44 CSS pixels using padding or min-width/min-height to satisfy WCAG 2.5.5.

wcag_dictionary.description: Web content does not restrict use of input modalities available on a platform except where the restriction is essential, required to ensure the security of the content, or required to respect user settings.
common_failures: Application disables touch input when a keyboard is connected
Form restricts paste from clipboard on password fields
App detects touch and removes keyboard shortcut hints permanently
Content forces mouse-only interaction when a stylus is available
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for users who switch between input modalities (keyboard, touch, mouse, switch access) depending on their context or disability.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Connect multiple input devices (keyboard + touch). Verify the application does not disable or restrict any input modality based on the presence of another.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's app restricts input modalities. Generate JavaScript that detects and supports all available input methods simultaneously without restricting any, to satisfy WCAG 2.5.6.

wcag_dictionary.description: All functionality that uses a dragging movement for operation can be achieved by a single pointer without dragging, unless dragging is essential. (New in WCAG 2.2)
common_failures: Sortable lists only reorderable via drag-and-drop with no keyboard or button alternative
Sliders that require dragging with no click-to-position or keyboard step support
File upload areas that only support drag-and-drop with no browse button
Kanban boards with drag-only card movement
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical for users with motor disabilities who cannot perform dragging gestures but can perform single-pointer taps or clicks.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Identify all drag interactions. Verify each has an equivalent single-pointer (click/tap) or keyboard alternative that achieves the same result.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's draggable list or slider has no single-pointer alternative. Generate HTML/JS adding Up/Down buttons for list reordering and click-to-position for sliders to satisfy WCAG 2.5.7.

wcag_dictionary.description: The size of the target for pointer inputs is at least 24 by 24 CSS pixels, except where spacing, equivalent, inline, essential, or user-agent control applies. (New in WCAG 2.2)
common_failures: Icon buttons with a hit area below 24×24 CSS pixels
Densely packed navigation links with targets smaller than 24×24 px
Close or dismiss icons smaller than 24×24 px with no surrounding spacing
wcag_dictionary.note: New in WCAG 2.2. Less strict than 2.5.5 (AAA 44×44px). Targets smaller than 24×24 CSS pixels may still pass if sufficient spacing (offset) exists around them.
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for users with motor disabilities and elderly users who need adequately sized touch or click targets.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Inspect interactive targets in DevTools. Verify each has a bounding box of at least 24×24 CSS pixels, or that targets smaller than this have sufficient offset spacing from adjacent targets.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user has interactive targets below 24×24 CSS pixels. Generate CSS increasing padding or adding a minimum size to bring all targets to at least 24×24 CSS pixels to satisfy WCAG 2.5.8.

wcag_dictionary.description: The default human language of each web page can be programmatically determined.

act_rules.title: HTML page has lang attribute
HTML page lang attribute has valid language tag

common_failures: Missing lang attribute on <html> element
lang attribute with invalid value (e.g., lang='english')
lang attribute uses wrong BCP 47 tag
lang attribute exists but is empty
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical — screen readers select the pronunciation engine based on lang; a missing or wrong value causes garbled speech output in the user's native language.
affected_users: Users with cognitive disabilities relying on text-to-speech
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Inspect the opening <html> tag. Verify a valid BCP 47 language tag is present in the lang attribute.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user provided an HTML document missing a valid language definition. Generate a code snippet adding the correct 'lang' attribute (e.g., lang='en') to the <html> tag to satisfy WCAG 3.1.1.

wcag_dictionary.description: The human language of each passage or phrase in the content can be programmatically determined except for proper names, technical terms, and indeterminate language words.

act_rules.title: HTML element language subtag matches default language

common_failures: A page in English contains a French paragraph without lang='fr' on that element
Multilingual navigation menu items lack individual lang attributes
Foreign language quotations lack appropriate lang attribute
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious — screen readers mispronounce foreign language passages if not marked with the correct lang attribute.
affected_users: Screen reader users in multilingual contexts
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Identify any passages in a different language than the page default. Verify they carry a lang attribute with a valid BCP 47 language tag.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user has a multilingual page with foreign language passages lacking lang attributes. Generate corrected HTML wrapping foreign passages with appropriate lang attributes to satisfy WCAG 3.1.2.

wcag_dictionary.description: A mechanism is available for identifying specific definitions of words or phrases used in an unusual or restricted way, including idioms and jargon.
common_failures: Technical jargon used without definition or glossary link
Idioms used without explanation for non-native speakers
Domain-specific abbreviations expanded only on first use without a glossary
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for users with cognitive disabilities, low literacy, and non-native language speakers who may not understand specialised or unusual word usage.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Identify jargon, idioms, and unusual word usage. Verify a definition or glossary is accessible for each, either inline, via tooltip, or in a linked glossary.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's content contains jargon without definitions. Generate HTML using <dfn> elements, aria-describedby tooltips, or a linked glossary section to identify unusual words and satisfy WCAG 3.1.3.

wcag_dictionary.description: A mechanism for identifying the expanded form or meaning of abbreviations is available.
common_failures: Abbreviations used with no expansion on first occurrence
Abbreviations in headings with no explanation
Acronyms not linked to a glossary or wrapped in <abbr>
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for users with cognitive disabilities, low literacy, and non-native language speakers who cannot interpret abbreviations.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Identify all abbreviations and acronyms. Verify each is expanded using <abbr title=''>, defined in a glossary, or expanded on first use.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's content uses unexplained abbreviations. Generate HTML wrapping abbreviations in <abbr title='Full Form'> elements and adding a glossary section to satisfy WCAG 3.1.4.

wcag_dictionary.description: When text requires reading ability more advanced than the lower secondary education level after removal of proper names and titles, supplemental content or a version that does not require reading ability more advanced than the lower secondary education level is available.
common_failures: Legal or medical content written at a high reading level with no plain-language summary
Technical documentation with no simplified explanation
Government forms with complex language and no plain-English alternative
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for users with cognitive disabilities, low literacy, and non-native language speakers who cannot comprehend advanced reading levels.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Assess the Flesch-Kincaid or equivalent reading level of the content. If above lower secondary level (approximately grade 7–9), verify a simplified summary or plain-language version is available.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's content is written at a high reading level. Generate a plain-language HTML summary section alongside the original content to satisfy WCAG 3.1.5.

wcag_dictionary.description: A mechanism is available for identifying specific pronunciation of words where meaning of the words, in context, is ambiguous without knowing the pronunciation.
common_failures: Homographs used without phonetic guidance (e.g., 'lead' the metal vs 'lead' the verb)
Foreign words used without pronunciation guide
Names that are ambiguous in pronunciation without clarification
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for screen reader users and users who rely on text-to-speech where mispronunciation causes misunderstanding.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Identify words whose pronunciation determines meaning. Verify each is clarified via phonetic transcription, ruby annotation, or a linked pronunciation guide.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's content contains ambiguous homographs. Generate HTML using ruby annotations or aria-label to clarify pronunciation and satisfy WCAG 3.1.6.

wcag_dictionary.description: Any component does not initiate a change of context when it receives focus.
common_failures: A select menu opens a new page when an option receives focus (before the user confirms)
A form field submits the form when it receives focus
A tooltip or popup opens a new browser window on focus
Focus moving to a component triggers a page reload
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious — unexpected context changes on focus disorient keyboard and screen reader users who are simply navigating through the page.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Tab through all interactive elements. Verify that no context change (new window, page reload, form submission, significant DOM change) occurs merely by receiving focus.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's select element or component triggers a page change on focus. Generate corrected HTML/JS that defers the action until the user explicitly activates the control (e.g., presses Enter or clicks a Submit button) to satisfy WCAG 3.2.1.

wcag_dictionary.description: Changing the setting of any user interface component does not automatically cause a change of context unless the user has been advised of the behavior before using the component.
common_failures: Selecting a radio button immediately navigates to a new page
Choosing from a dropdown/select immediately submits the form
Checking a checkbox triggers a full page reload without warning
Entering text in a search field navigates away before the user finishes typing
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious — automatic context changes on input prevent users from correcting mistakes or reviewing their input before submission.
affected_users: Users with motor disabilities who make accidental inputs
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Change the value of all form controls (radio, checkbox, select, text input). Verify no context change occurs automatically. If it does, confirm advance notice was given before the control.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's select element auto-submits on change. Generate corrected HTML adding an explicit Submit button and removing the onchange navigation handler to satisfy WCAG 3.2.2.

wcag_dictionary.description: Navigational mechanisms that are repeated on multiple web pages within a set of web pages occur in the same relative order each time they are repeated, unless a change is initiated by the user.
common_failures: Primary navigation order changes between pages without user action
Breadcrumb appears before the navigation on some pages and after on others
Search field moves from the header to the footer on certain page types
Footer link order varies inconsistently across pages
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for users with cognitive disabilities and screen reader users who build a mental model of the page structure and rely on consistent navigation placement.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Compare the navigation block order across at least 3 different page types in the site. Verify the relative order of repeated navigation elements is identical.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's site has inconsistent navigation order across pages. Generate a shared HTML navigation component with a fixed relative order to be used across all page templates, satisfying WCAG 3.2.3.

wcag_dictionary.description: Components that have the same functionality within a set of web pages are identified consistently.
common_failures: A search button is labelled 'Search' on one page and 'Find' on another
The same icon is used for different functions on different pages
A print function uses different accessible names ('Print page' vs 'Print this')
Breadcrumb component has different aria-label values across pages
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for users with cognitive disabilities who depend on consistent labels to recognise and predict the behaviour of repeated components.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Identify components with the same function across pages. Verify their text labels and accessible names are identical. Cross-check with a site-wide accessibility audit.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user has identical functionality components with inconsistent labels across pages. Generate a component naming reference or corrected HTML ensuring the accessible name is identical wherever the component appears, satisfying WCAG 3.2.4.

wcag_dictionary.description: Changes of context are initiated only by user request or a mechanism is available to turn off such changes.
common_failures: Auto-refreshing pages without user request
Links that open in new windows without warning
Any automatic redirect or page change not initiated by the user
wcag_dictionary.impact: Critical for users with cognitive disabilities and screen reader users who are disoriented by unexpected context changes.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Verify no context change (new window, page reload, significant DOM change) occurs without user initiation. If it does, confirm a disable mechanism exists.
wcag_dictionary.featherless_prompt_context: The user's site auto-refreshes or opens new windows unexpectedly. Generate HTML/JS ensuring all context changes are user-initiated, with a warning for new windows using rel='noopener' and an aria-label notice to satisfy WCAG 3.2.5.

wcag_dictionary.description: If a web page contains any of the following help mechanisms, and those mechanisms are repeated on multiple pages, they occur in the same relative order to other page content on each page: human contact details, human contact mechanism, self-help option, a fully automated contact mechanism. (New in WCAG 2.2)
common_failures: Chat widget appears in the header on some pages and the footer on others
Help link is in the nav on some pages and the sidebar on others
Contact information moves position across page templates
wcag_dictionary.impact: Serious for users with cognitive disabilities who rely on consistent placement of help mechanisms across pages.
wcag_dictionary.test_procedure: Identify all help mechanisms. Compare their relative position in the page across multiple page types. Verify placement is consistent.
